title: Cyanea habenata
taxonomy: Cyanea habenata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyanea and is native to the Hawaiian Islands. This species is a type of flowering plant that produces showy, bell-shaped flowers in shades of blue and purple. Cyanea habenata is considered to be an endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation. The classification of Cyanea habenata is further detailed in the binomial nomenclature system, where it is identified as Cyanea habenata (Hillebrand).
uses: Cyanea habenata is native to Hawaii and its flowers are used in traditional medicine. The plant's flowers have been used to treat various ailments, including fever and sore throats. Cyanea habenata is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.
